headline: Is Marriott International (MAR) Overvalued Following Its First Branded Apartment Rental Project?
summary: Marriott International (MAR) is drawing investor attention after announcing its first branded apartment rentals within the planned W Cleveland project, a $218 million mixed-use property that is expected to combine hotel rooms and long-term rental apartments. See our latest analysis for Marriott International. Recent moves like the W Cleveland project come as Marriott International’s share price has eased, with a 30 day share price return down 7.39% and a 90 day share price return down 2.27%...

headline: AEP Names David Marriott and Charles Meyers to Board of Directors
summary: American Electric Power (Nasdaq: AEP) today announced that David Marriott, Chairman of the Board of Marriott International, and Charles Meyers, Executive Chairman and former President and Chief Executive Officer of Equinix, have been elected to AEP's Board of Directors (the "Board"), effective July 20.

raw.headline: AEP Names David Marriott and Charles Meyers to Board of Directors

headline: JP Morgan Maintains Neutral on Marriott International, Raises Price Target to $400
summary: JP Morgan  analyst Daniel Politzer   maintains Marriott International (NASDAQ:MAR) with a Neutral and raises the price target from $387 to $400.

headline: TD Cowen Maintains Buy on Marriott International, Raises Price Target to $420
summary: TD Cowen  analyst Kevin Kopelman   maintains Marriott International (NASDAQ:MAR) with a Buy and raises the price target from $410 to $420.

headline: Marriott Launches Branded Apartment Rentals in Cleveland
summary: Marriott brings branded apartment rentals to Cleveland with W Cleveland, its first W Hotels project focused on luxury rental living.
